(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

I'm not against this specific patch landing, but I think we could find other bugs and regressions if e2fsck and/or LFSCK was run regularly during testing.

The http://review.whamcloud.com/16237 patch could be improved to include running e2fsck on the target filesystems before running lfsck, so that we can check this during testing.  Running once after every test script is useful, but it might also be useful to have an option run it after every (or specified) subtests, like:

 FSCK=y
 SANITY_FSCK=y
 CONF_SANITY_FSCK="83 84 85"

to mean "run fsck after every test", or "run fsck after sanity.sh" or "run e2fsck after conf-sanity test_83, test_84, and test_85".

Patch Set 4:

> FSCK=y
> SANITY_FSCK=y
> CONF_SANITY_FSCK="83 84 85"
> to mean "run fsck after every test", or "run fsck after sanity.sh" or "run e2fsck after conf-sanity test_83, test_84, and test_85".

This can be as port of the improvement in the future. But for this special case, we need run e2fsck during the test_84 to verify whether there is some disk corruption before the MDT failover. I will update the patch as suggested.
Uploaded patch set 5.

parents.subject: LU-7377 utils: Don't fail on plugin load for mount/tunefs

commit.subject: LU-7169 tests: check disk corruption during failover
commit.message:
LU-7169 tests: check disk corruption during failover

It is a debug patch for conf-sanity test_84. It is suspected
that there is some disk corruption during the MDT0 failover.

Test-Parameters: mdsfilesystemtype=ldiskfs mdtfilesystemtype=ldiskfs ostfilesystemtype=ldiskfs testlist=conf-sanity,conf-sanity,conf-sanity
